title: CloudFront OAC, Private S3 Origins, and SSE-KMS · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds private origin authorization contract, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of private origin authorization contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9115 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, distinguish regular s3 origins from website endpoints by https, oac, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop S3 Origin Website Boundary, keep S3 Origin Website Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
